title: Cache Identity, TTL, Clock Injection, Readonly Copies, and Capacity · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for cache identity, ttl, clock injection, readonly copies, and capacity and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for observable bounded TypeScript resource cache. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
Inject a clock and define fresh, stale, expired, and boundary instants without timer sleeps in unit tests.
Use immutable domain values, controlled cloning, or copy-on-read so consumers cannot rewrite cached or historical data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 2023 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile canonical TTL cache decisions, runtime ownership, error policy, and capacity eviction; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one compiler, Node runtime, emitted artifact, package, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound entries and approximate bytes, implement deterministic eviction policy, and expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, bypass, and load decisions.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound entries and approximate bytes, implement deterministic eviction policy, and expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, bypass, and load decisions.
